title: UCS X-Series Intelligent Fabric Module (IFM) Health (UCSM, UCSX-9508)
exclusions: In scope: Cisco UCSM-managed UCSX-9508 X-Series chassis with paired UCSX-I-9108-25G or UCSX-I-9108-100G Intelligent Fabric Modules (IFMs), polled by Splunk_TA_cisco_ucs (Splunkbase 2731) into canonical cisco:ucs:stats, cisco:ucs:inventory, and cisco:ucs:faults sourcetypes — the equipmentChassis model match on UCSX-9508 is what guards this UC against being misfired by sibling UC data in the same index. Excludes UC-19.1.5 (Fabric Interconnect northbound LAG to upstream Nexus or other switch on the ^fabric/lan/(A|B)/pc- tree — that is the FI-to-spine story, not the chassis-IFM slot tree). Excludes UC-19.1.13 (per-FI Ethernet member CRC, FCS, and discard-velocity on sys/switch-…/…-port-…; FI-side error-velocity, not the chassis IFM oper or fault narrative). Excludes UC-19.1.16 (B-series UCSB-5108 IOM-to-FI uplink utilisation percent on classic IOM dn families — same etherIom* counter MOs but anchored on the B-series chassis tree, not X9508). Excludes UC-19.1.6 (I/O card environmental, power, and thermal emphasis without the operState, fault, fabric LAG end-point, and firm-pair story this UC ladders together). Excludes UC-19.1.22 (Intersight or IMM power, thermal, and policy plane — that path is correct for IMM-led X-series domains; this UC is intentionally UCSM XML/HTTPS classic sourcetypes only and will look thin under IMM). Excludes standalone Cisco UCS C-series rack servers managed only by CIMC without a UCSM chassis association (no equipmentChassis under sys/chassis-* and therefore no IFM dn tree). Excludes Cisco HyperFlex clusters where the HyperFlex control plane is the authoritative source of truth for chassis component health and the cisco:ucs:stats path is incomplete by design. Out of scope: third-party blade chassis (HPE Synergy, Dell PowerEdge MX) and any non-Cisco modular system — those have separate cat-19 UCs.
dataSources: Index `cisco_ucs` (or your org index) and sourcetypes: `cisco:ucs:stats` from the Cisco UCS statistics modular input (classId set must include at least equipmentChassis, equipmentIOCard, equipmentIOCardStats, etherPIo, etherIomStats, etherIomTxStats, etherIomRxStats, fabricEthLanEp, fabricEthLanEpStats, firmwareRunning for this UC). `cisco:ucs:inventory` for equipmentChassis model and firmwareRunning strings. `cisco:ucs:faults` for faultInst-style rows with dn and severity. `cisco:ucs:events` (optional) for descr and affected_dn. Mechanism: Splunk_TA_cisco_ucs ciscoucs:// with stats_classes, fault_interval, event_interval, inventory_interval, and enable_* flags per your 2731 build.
app: `Splunk_TA_cisco_ucs` ([Splunkbase 2731](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/2731), canonical app directory id uses an underscore, not a hyphen).
spl:
index=cisco_ucs (sourcetype="cisco:ucs:stats" OR sourcetype="cisco:ucs:faults" OR sourcetype="cisco:ucs:inventory") earliest=-4h
| eval cidl=lower(coalesce(classId, class_id, ""))
| eval st=lower(sourcetype)
| where (st="cisco:ucs:stats" AND cidl IN ("equipmentiocard","equipmentiocardstats","etherpio","fabricethlanepstats","fabricethlanep","firmwarerunning","etheriomstats","etheriomtxstats","etheriomrxstats")) OR st="cisco:ucs:faults" OR (st="cisco:ucs:inventory" AND cidl IN ("equipmentchassis","firmwarerunning","equipmentiocard"))
| rex field=dn "(?i)^sys/chassis-(?<chassis_id>\d+)"
| where isnotnull(chassis_id) AND (st!="cisco:ucs:faults" OR match(dn, "(?i)^sys/chassis-\d+/slot-[12]"))
| join type=inner max=0 chassis_id
    [ search index=cisco_ucs sourcetype="cisco:ucs:inventory" earliest=-24h
      | eval c2=lower(coalesce(classId, class_id, "")) | where c2="equipmentchassis"
      | eval mdl2=coalesce(model, mdl, "") | where match(mdl2, "(?i)UCSX-9508")
      | rex field=dn "(?i)^sys/chassis-(?<chassis_id>\d+)" | table chassis_id | dedup chassis_id ]
| join type=left max=0 chassis_id
    [ search index=cisco_ucs (sourcetype="cisco:ucs:stats" OR sourcetype="cisco:ucs:inventory") earliest=-24h
      | eval cidl0=lower(coalesce(classId, class_id, "")) | where cidl0="firmwarerunning"
      | rex field=dn "(?i)^sys/chassis-(?<chassis_id>\d+)"
      | where match(dn, "(?i)/slot-[12]/")
      | eval s=if(match(dn, "(?i)/slot-1/"),"A", if(match(dn, "(?i)/slot-2/"),"B", null()))
      | where s IN ("A","B")
      | eval fw0=coalesce(version, v_version, running_version, "")
      | sort 0 chassis_id, s, -_time
      | dedup chassis_id, s
      | stats first(fw0) as fwa by chassis_id, s
      | eval A=if(s=="A",fwa,null())
      | eval B=if(s=="B",fwa,null())
      | stats max(A) as A max(B) as B by chassis_id
      | eval firm_drift=if(isnotnull(A) AND isnotnull(B) AND A!="" AND B!="" AND A!=B,1,0)
      | table chassis_id, A, B, firm_drift ]
| eval op=lower(trim(coalesce(operState, oper_state, "")))
| eval pr=lower(trim(coalesce(presence, "")))
| eval iocard_bad=if(cidl="equipmentiocard" AND ((isnotnull(op) AND op!="" AND op!="operable") OR (isnotnull(pr) AND pr!="" AND pr!="equipped")),1,0)
| eval fsev=lower(trim(coalesce(severity, "normal")))
| eval fault_sev=if(st="cisco:ucs:faults",fsev,"")
| eval fault_code=coalesce(code, "")
| eval fault_hit=if(st="cisco:ucs:faults" AND fsev!="" AND fsev!="cleared" AND fsev!="info",1,0)
| eval pio_op=if(cidl="etherpio", lower(trim(coalesce(operState, oper_state, ""))), null())
| sort 0 dn _time
| streamstats current=f last(pio_op) as pio_op_prev by dn
| eval port_flap=if(cidl="etherpio" AND isnotnull(pio_op) AND isnotnull(pio_op_prev) AND pio_op!=pio_op_prev,1,0)
| eval felev=if(cidl="fabricethlanep" OR cidl="fabricethlanepstats", lower(trim(coalesce(operState, oper_state, ""))), null())
| eval fe_bad=if((cidl="fabricethlanep" OR cidl="fabricethlanepstats") AND isnotnull(felev) AND felev!="" AND !match(felev, "^(up|link-up|online|operable|unknown)$"),1,0)
| eval iom_row=if(cidl IN ("etheriomstats","etheriomtxstats","etheriomrxstats"),1,0)
| eval watch_env=if(cidl="equipmentiocardstats" OR iom_row=1,1,0)
| eval severity_bucket=case(iocard_bad=1 OR fe_bad=1 OR match(fault_sev,"^(critical|fatal)"),"critical", fault_hit=1 OR coalesce(firm_drift,0)=1 OR port_flap=1,"warning", watch_env=1,"watch",1=1,"ok")
| table _time, chassis_id, st, cidl, dn, op, pr, fault_code, fault_sev, pio_op, felev, port_flap, iocard_bad, fe_bad, firm_drift, severity_bucket
| sort 0 - _time, severity_bucket
description: UCSM-managed Cisco UCSX-9508 X-Series IFM (Intelligent Fabric Module) health: raises when equipmentIOCard operState or presence is off the expected operable/equipped baseline, cisco:ucs:faults affect sys/chassis-*/slot-1|2 under the IFM tree, etherPIo or fabricEthLanEp* oper does not look up on the chassis-fabric path, streamstats on etherPIo flags rapid oper flips, firmwareRunning differs between slot-1 and slot-2 IFM for the same chassis, and optional etherIom* rows surface watch-tier line-rate context on UCSX-I-9108-25G and UCSX-I-9108-100G, using cisco:ucs:stats, cisco:ucs:faults, and cisco:ucs:inventory only.
value: Cisco’s UCS X-Series is the modular blade line many buyers pick for new compute instead of legacy B-series; the IFM pair is the chassis-side equivalent of the old IOM, but the MO names and the ops story are an availability and fault class problem before a northbound LAG (19.1.5) or FI CRC (19.1.13) blinks. The business outcome is a single, chassis-scoped triage that infrastructure and platform teams can action together, instead of an application-only incident with no line of sight to the IFM pair that every node in the X9508 shares.
implementation: Deploy Splunk_TA_cisco_ucs (2731) with ciscoucs:// inputs for stats, faults, events, and inventory. Paste the exact stats_classes line from this use case, prove classId with | stats in Search, then save the included spl on a 5–10m schedule with throttles; add optional cisco:ucs:events and ucs_xseries_chassis.csv for labels after the first green run.
visualization: Single value tiles: counts for severity_bucket critical+warning, firm_drift=1, and 4h IFM-impacted rows. Timechart: port_flap and fault count by chassis_id. Bar chart: severity_bucket by chassis. Sortable table: time, chassis_id, dn, cidl, pio_op, felev, fault_code, firm_drift, severity_bucket. Optional heatmap: chassis by IFM slot for 24h planning. Line chart: optional cisco:ucs:events rate for war-room text.

references.title: Cisco UCS X9508 Chassis data sheet (X-Series, IFM context)
Cisco IFM installation (X9508, Intelligent Fabric Module slots)
Cisco UCS Manager — developer and XML API hub

knownFalsePositives: (1) Planned IFM firmware rolling wave: one leg can be newer than the other until the second activation finishes, so firm_drift=1 mid-change; track the change id, not an outage. (2) Scheduled A or B IFM swap during a hardware refresh: operState and presence pass through transient values; the maintenance ticket is authoritative. (3) Admin-down or maintenance on a vNIC or fabric path: oper can be non-operable for the change window; use a maintenance lookup. (4) DAC reseat or PXE kick causing short etherPIo oper flips: use two transitions in 15m before a production page, or a post-change tag. (5) Staggered IFM-A/IFM-B file-by-file activation during a rolling wave: drift is the expected half-way state. (6) UCSX-I-9108-25G to UCSX-I-9108-100G uplift: versions differ on purpose until both slots match the target BOM. (7) TAC-expected fault F0408 during a guided reseat: keep the TAC case id in the ticket, not a second P1, unless a second health signal also fires. (8) Poll skew: a fault cleared in the UCSM GUI can miss one cisco:ucs:faults poll; widen earliest or add cisco:ucs:events. (9) Chassis renumber, lab motion, or DR that reused chassis- ids: the equipmentChassis join can look empty until inventory republishes. (10) One IFM not yet cabled: firm_drift is not computable; suppress drift. (11) A widened test filter that pulls UCSB-5108 data: only the UCSX-9508 model join keeps B-series out; fix the test, not the TA. (12) Intersight- or IMM-primary domains: thin cisco:ucs:stats is a 19.1.22 and governance sign, not proof the IFM is healthy.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites (read in order)
Scope: the **Cisco UCS X-Series Intelligent Fabric Module (IFM)** in **UCSX-9508** (IFM models **UCSX-I-9108-25G** or **UCSX-I-9108-100G**) is the card pair every rear slot uses to reach the **Fabric Interconnects**; this use case is UCSM XML/HTTPS to canonical `cisco:ucs:stats` / `cisco:ucs:inventory` / `cisco:ucs:faults` only, never a renamed “xseries” stub sourcetype.
• Splunk Add-on for Cisco UCS (canonical app id Splunk_TA_cisco_ucs, Splunkbase 2731) 4.2+ class releases on a Heavy Forwarder (or any host allowed to run modular inputs) with outbound HTTPS 443 to the UCSM cluster VIP and full TLS trust chain in the splunkd trust store. Do not use a private CA without importing the full chain: the add-on will look like 401, 403, or connection resets before you ever get to SPL mistakes.
• A read-only UCSM API user whose role can read the Equipment tree for the UCSX-9508 X-Series chassis, IFM objects under sys/chassis-N/slot-1 and sys/chassis-N/slot-2, LAN fabric LAGs, and inventory and fault streams. Do not use a human administrator’s primary credential; rotate a dedicated `splunk_ucs` style account the same way you do for vCenter readers.
• Index: examples use cisco_ucs. The sourcetype must remain the add-on defaults (cisco:ucs:stats, cisco:ucs:inventory, cisco:ucs:faults, and optionally cisco:ucs:events in older lab notes) so this library, your props, and the on-call runbook match. If the TA prints cisco:ucs:stats, do not add a “pretty” cisco:ucs:ifm name in dashboards — that is a 0-event trap for new scrollers.
• This use case is UCSM-managed X-Series only. Intersight or IMM-primary domains can stop exposing the classic class poll; treat empty cisco:ucs:stats in those domains as a data-source design issue, and pair with the Intersight-shaped sibling UC-19.1.22 for power, thermal, and policy, not a silent “all clear.”
• Sibling scoping: UC-19.1.16 is B-series IOM (or the same IOM-stat class names on non-X chassis) uplink utilisation percent. UC-19.1.5 is northbound LAG to Nexus on the fabric/lan/…/pc- tree. UC-19.1.13 is per-FI CRC and error-velocity, not the chassis IFM. UC-19.1.6 is a broader I/O-card environmental set; 19.1.25 still uses equipmentIOCardStats for context but the outcome is not “sensors as the only KPI.” You must be able to tell UCSB-5108 from UCSX-9508: the chassis/slot/fabric/port dn family looks similar, so the model filter on equipmentChassis is the guard rail.
• You should sample five live events in Search (head) and keep a scratch note of every alternate spelling you see: operState vs oper_state, classId vs class_id, and version on firmwareRunning, because 2731 minor builds sometimes rename keys before the docs do.

**Step 1 — Configure data collection**

(1) Install Splunk_TA_cisco_ucs on the collection node and the search tier, following Splunk 9.2+ and Splunk Cloud support guidance for app 2731. Install full CA trust on the same OS account that runs splunkd; corporate TLS intercepts break UCSM if you only trust a browser.
(2) In Splunk, open **Settings → Data inputs** and locate the ciscoucs:// style Cisco UCS modular input. Wire index, host, xml_url (NUOVA: typically https://<ucsm_fqdn>/nuova for stats/faults/inventory) and the credential object in the secret store, not clear text in a repo. Never merge default/ by hand: use local/ or an app overlay your Deployment Server already owns.
(3) Author $SPLUNK_HOME/etc/apps/Splunk_TA_cisco_ucs/local/inputs.conf (or a deployment app) so enable_stats, enable_faults, enable_events, and enable_inventory all match the operational contract below. Polling: stats polling_interval 300s is a common starting point; inventory_interval 1200s is a common 20-minute inventory refresh. Faults and events at 60s give faster fault-raise/close; wider intervals save API load. Split heavy lab domains onto a read-only second poll only after License Usage and _internal show volume you can pay for.
(4) Exact stats_classes line for this IFM health use case: include **equipmentChassis** (to prove UCSX-9508 model in inventory and avoid mixing B- and X-series in the same alert), equipmentIOCard and equipmentIOCardStats, etherPIo, etherIomStats, etherIomTxStats, etherIomRxStats, fabricEthLanEp, fabricEthLanEpStats, and firmwareRunning. The etherIom* set is the same per-port counter namespace B-series 19.1.16 uses, but the dn still lands under the X9508 slot tree. fabricEthLanEp* covers the chassis-fabric LAG end-point; etherPIo covers physical port opers. Optional expansion on UCSX-I-9108-100G: keep a side capacity panel for new counter MOs — do not merge that util story with this health ladder.

```ini
[ciscoucs://prod_ucsm_x_ifm]
index = cisco_ucs
disabled = 0
xml_url = https://ucsm.example.com/nuova
# username and password: set in the Splunk credentials UI, not in git
enable_stats = 1
polling_interval = 300
enable_faults = 1
fault_interval = 60
enable_events = 1
event_interval = 60
enable_inventory = 1
inventory_interval = 1200
verify_ssl = 1
stats_classes = equipmentChassis,equipmentIOCard,equipmentIOCardStats,etherPIo,etherIomStats,etherIomTxStats,etherIomRxStats,fabricEthLanEp,fabricEthLanEpStats,firmwareRunning
# equipmentChassis: X-only filter; equipmentIOCard* + ether*: IFM; fabricEthLanEp*: LAG endpoint; firmwareRunning: IFM A vs B
```

(5) After two full polling windows, run index=cisco_ucs sourcetype="cisco:ucs:stats" earliest=-1h | stats count by classId and expect equipmentIOCard, equipmentIOCardStats, etherPIo, and at least one etherIom* class. If a class is missing, the MO was not listed in stats_classes, or the UCSM account cannot read that subtree — fix the input before you tune the SPL. Also run index=cisco_ucs sourcetype="cisco:ucs:inventory" for equipmentChassis with model like UCSX-9508; if the model string is empty, the join in the spl will never return X series rows.
(6) Optional local lookup: lookups/ucs_xseries_chassis.csv in a small org-owned app, columns chassis_id,chassis_dn,chassis_model,notes, plus transforms.conf. The SPL in this use case does not require the file because it filters on the live equipmentChassis event; the CSV is for human site labels, executive dashboards, and CMDB back-fill in join panels.

**Step 2 — Create the search and alert**

(1) Copy the spl field in this sidecar into Search, set earliest=-4h, and run.

```spl
index=cisco_ucs (sourcetype="cisco:ucs:stats" OR sourcetype="cisco:ucs:faults" OR sourcetype="cisco:ucs:inventory") earliest=-4h
| eval cidl=lower(coalesce(classId, class_id, ""))
| eval st=lower(sourcetype)
| where (st="cisco:ucs:stats" AND cidl IN ("equipmentiocard","equipmentiocardstats","etherpio","fabricethlanepstats","fabricethlanep","firmwarerunning","etheriomstats","etheriomtxstats","etheriomrxstats")) OR st="cisco:ucs:faults" OR (st="cisco:ucs:inventory" AND cidl IN ("equipmentchassis","firmwarerunning","equipmentiocard"))
| rex field=dn "(?i)^sys/chassis-(?<chassis_id>\d+)"
| where isnotnull(chassis_id) AND (st!="cisco:ucs:faults" OR match(dn, "(?i)^sys/chassis-\d+/slot-[12]"))
| join type=inner max=0 chassis_id
    [ search index=cisco_ucs sourcetype="cisco:ucs:inventory" earliest=-24h
      | eval c2=lower(coalesce(classId, class_id, "")) | where c2="equipmentchassis"
      | eval mdl2=coalesce(model, mdl, "") | where match(mdl2, "(?i)UCSX-9508")
      | rex field=dn "(?i)^sys/chassis-(?<chassis_id>\d+)" | table chassis_id | dedup chassis_id ]
| join type=left max=0 chassis_id
    [ search index=cisco_ucs (sourcetype="cisco:ucs:stats" OR sourcetype="cisco:ucs:inventory") earliest=-24h
      | eval cidl0=lower(coalesce(classId, class_id, "")) | where cidl0="firmwarerunning"
      | rex field=dn "(?i)^sys/chassis-(?<chassis_id>\d+)"
      | where match(dn, "(?i)/slot-[12]/")
      | eval s=if(match(dn, "(?i)/slot-1/"),"A", if(match(dn, "(?i)/slot-2/"),"B", null()))
      | where s IN ("A","B")
      | eval fw0=coalesce(version, v_version, running_version, "")
      | sort 0 chassis_id, s, -_time
      | dedup chassis_id, s
      | stats first(fw0) as fwa by chassis_id, s
      | eval A=if(s=="A",fwa,null())
      | eval B=if(s=="B",fwa,null())
      | stats max(A) as A max(B) as B by chassis_id
      | eval firm_drift=if(isnotnull(A) AND isnotnull(B) AND A!="" AND B!="" AND A!=B,1,0)
      | table chassis_id, A, B, firm_drift ]
| eval op=lower(trim(coalesce(operState, oper_state, "")))
| eval pr=lower(trim(coalesce(presence, "")))
| eval iocard_bad=if(cidl="equipmentiocard" AND ((isnotnull(op) AND op!="" AND op!="operable") OR (isnotnull(pr) AND pr!="" AND pr!="equipped")),1,0)
| eval fsev=lower(trim(coalesce(severity, "normal")))
| eval fault_sev=if(st="cisco:ucs:faults",fsev,"")
| eval fault_code=coalesce(code, "")
| eval fault_hit=if(st="cisco:ucs:faults" AND fsev!="" AND fsev!="cleared" AND fsev!="info",1,0)
| eval pio_op=if(cidl="etherpio", lower(trim(coalesce(operState, oper_state, ""))), null())
| sort 0 dn _time
| streamstats current=f last(pio_op) as pio_op_prev by dn
| eval port_flap=if(cidl="etherpio" AND isnotnull(pio_op) AND isnotnull(pio_op_prev) AND pio_op!=pio_op_prev,1,0)
| eval felev=if(cidl="fabricethlanep" OR cidl="fabricethlanepstats", lower(trim(coalesce(operState, oper_state, ""))), null())
| eval fe_bad=if((cidl="fabricethlanep" OR cidl="fabricethlanepstats") AND isnotnull(felev) AND felev!="" AND !match(felev, "^(up|link-up|online|operable|unknown)$"),1,0)
| eval iom_row=if(cidl IN ("etheriomstats","etheriomtxstats","etheriomrxstats"),1,0)
| eval watch_env=if(cidl="equipmentiocardstats" OR iom_row=1,1,0)
| eval severity_bucket=case(iocard_bad=1 OR fe_bad=1 OR match(fault_sev,"^(critical|fatal)"),"critical", fault_hit=1 OR coalesce(firm_drift,0)=1 OR port_flap=1,"warning", watch_env=1,"watch",1=1,"ok")
| table _time, chassis_id, st, cidl, dn, op, pr, fault_code, fault_sev, pio_op, felev, port_flap, iocard_bad, fe_bad, firm_drift, severity_bucket
| sort 0 - _time, severity_bucket
```

The SPL above is the exact copy from the `spl` field; keep them in sync if you edit either. You should see rows with cidl in lower form (equipmentiocard, etherpio, fabricethlanep, firmwarerunning) after the inner join. If the join to UCSX-9508 chassis is empty, you are not in a UCSM-managed X9508 domain, inventory is not reaching Splunk, or the model string in UCSM is not the UCSX-9508 string you expect — re-sample equipmentChassis.
(2) The pipeline: inner join keeps only chassis_id values that have an equipmentChassis row whose model matches UCSX-9508, which stops UCSB-5108 IOM traffic from being mistaken for X IFM traffic when both live in the same index. A left join adds firm_drift from a firmwareRunning subsearch: latest string per slot-1 and slot-2, compared as plain text. If you need a structured bundle compare (major, minor, maintenance), that becomes a small Python external alert — keep Splunk in string form first so every admin can read it in the job inspector.
(3) The equipmentIOCard branch flags non-operable and non-equipped states: tune with your SRE after you see real quiesce states; some maintenance modes legitimately set oper to admin-down, which should land in a change-calendar suppression table, not a 03:00 page. The cisco:ucs:faults branch is limited to dn under sys/chassis-*/slot-1|2 to stay on the IFM backplane, not on random FI faults. The etherPIo streamstats pair is a stand-in for “did oper flip sample-to-sample on the same dn?” — it is a proxy for flap, not a counter delta like 19.1.16 util.
(4) The fabricEthLanEp and fabricEthLanEpStats operState is evaluated for “not clearly up or online” style strings; if your build emits link-up, treat that as good by extending the match list in a governed change, not a silent one-line local edit. severity_bucket is four rungs: critical, warning, watch, ok. For paging, | where severity_bucket in ("critical","warning") and throttle on chassis_id plus a regex on dn for the slot. Keep watch in dashboards until your noise floor is trusted.
(5) Companion: enable cisco:ucs:events with the same ciscoucs:// event_interval if you want human descr text in a war room; keep it as a second panel, not a forked copy of the detection.
(6) HBM: newer UCSX-I-9108-100G builds can expose more metrics in UCSM; if your five-event sample shows an extra classId, add it to stats_classes, wait two polls, then add a “capacity/observability” child panel, not a second P1 to the on-call for every counter tick.

**Step 3 — Validate**

(a) In UCSM GUI: Equipment, Chassis, open a UCSX-9508, and verify the IFM in slot-1 and slot-2, their models (UCSX-I-9108-25G vs UCSX-I-9108-100G), and the fabric A/B path in the same 15–30 minutes as Splunk’s window. Sibling call-out for auditors: 19.1.5 answers whether the Northbound LAG to Nexus is up; 19.1.16 answers utilisation to the FIs for B IOM/etherIom math; 19.1.25 answers this chassis IFM’s health, faults, LAG end-point, and firm-pair. They are not interchangeable green lights.
(b) 19.1.13 remains FI per-port error ramp; 25 remains chassis-IFM. A green 13 and green 5 can co-exist with a red 25, because the fault may be in the X IFM or its optics before a FI CRC moves.
(c) 19.1.22 remains Intersight/IMM for server power and platform policy; 25 is intentionally UCSM XML. If the domain is IMM-led and classic stats are empty, the correct story is a governance and ingestion plan, not a hunt for a non-existent sourcetype rename.
(d) 19.1.6 is environmental density on card stats; 25 still reads the same class for “watch” and thermal context but the alert narrative is not “only temperature.”
(e) In Splunk, run: index=cisco_ucs sourcetype="cisco:ucs:stats" classId=equipmentIOCard earliest=-1h | head 5 and compare operState, presence, and model. Adjust coalesce() lists with what you see.
(f) C-series standalone on CIMC and Cisco HyperFlex nodes remain out of scope: there is no UCSX IFM in those stories; HX is owned by the HX control plane, not a generic cisco:ucs:stats pass.

**Step 4 — Operationalize**

• Row 1: single value tiles: count of critical and warning, plus firm_drift=1, plus a 4h “any IFM bad” count. Row 2: timechart of port_flap and fault count by chassis_id, separate series colours. Row 3: bar chart of severity_bucket counts by chassis_id. Row 4: a drillable table: _time, chassis_id, dn, cidl, pio_op, felev, fault_code, firm_drift, severity_bucket. Add a “copy dn” column for the UCSM deep link in your runbook. Row 5 (optional): heatmap chassis by slot-1/2 for long-running studies, not the first 4h of install day.
• Heat caps: a green utilisation in 19.1.16 does not make a red 25 a false problem — write that sentence into the PagerDuty run note so the network team does not close the case from upstream-only charts.
• RACI: platform owns IFM to FI optics home-run; data-centre networking owns the FI northbound. Put both on the first bridge when fe_bad=1 and fabricEthLanEp is involved.
• Evidence: for Cisco TAC, export the Splunk row, a UCSM Equipment screenshot, and, if the case is link level, a Nexus or FI counter screenshot from the same 15 minutes — never a single pane alone.
• If you add the optional lookup ucs_xseries_chassis.csv, join on chassis_id for a site column in the table so executives see a building name, not only chassis-3.

**Step 5 — Troubleshooting (nine X-Series IFM failure modes)**

(1) No cisco:ucs:stats: statistics off, 401, TLS, wrong index, or ciscoucs:// never deployed on this host. The first two discovery searches in Step 1 must work before a call to Splunk support. 2731 release notes: early 2.x families added or renamed MO classes; a forwarder on an older 1.x add-on that cannot list fabricEthLanEp* will never fabricate the rows.
(2) Mixed B- and X-series: someone removed the equipmentChassis join, or a migration copied both domains into the same index without tags. Re-run the model filter, then a rare manual where clause on dn org prefix.
(3) IMM-led X: telemetry path is not this UCSM poll; expect thin classic sourcetypes. Route to Intersight strategy and 19.1.22; do not keep an empty cisco:ucs:stats as “proof.”
(4) Rolling IFM upgrade: A!=B in firmware is expected mid-wave; add change and chassis_id to a suppression with an expiry time.
(5) No firmwareRunning in inventory: inventory_interval too long, MO not in stats, or the inventory modular input is disabled; fix inputs.conf, then wait two full inventory turns before judging drift=0.
(6) port_flap after DAC reseat, PXE, or a TAC RMA reseat: short burst of operState flips. Require two or three transitions in 15 minutes, or a change-id lookup, before paging. Vendor fault F0408 during a guided reseat can also appear: treat the TAC id as the system of record.
(7) UCSX-I-9108-100G “extra” HBM or counter MO never appears: UCSM/TA/BIOS line does not expose the MO yet, or the class is not in stats_classes. Sample five head events, add the new class, wait two stats polls, then a capacity panel, not a health page.
(8) Expected fault in UCSM, empty in Splunk: fault clear between polls, too narrow a time window, or a different code spelling. Widen earliest, and compare severity strings case-by-case. Intentional maintenance faults must align to the maintenance calendar.
(9) “Zero X in Splunk” but asset database says X exists: the cluster might be HyperFlex, standalone rack CIMC, or a read-only user without Equipment scope. Fix RBAC and domain type before RMA. Also verify UCS Manager version supports your X IFM on the FIs you have.

After you stabilise, keep 5, 13, 16, and 25 on one “UCS network three-axis” wall: northbound, FI errors, and chassis fabric module health. None replaces the other.
grandmaExplanation: We treat each new Cisco X cabinet like a small hub airport: two concourse bridges are the paired smart fabric cards every local plane uses to reach the long-haul network. We watch for closed gates, bumpy bridges, and mismatched playbooks, because if that hub wobbles, every flight through it delays at once, even when the main runway map still looks green.
hardware: Cisco UCSX-9508: 5RU modular chassis, eight compute slots, six fan modules, four PSUs, two IFM slots for UCSX-I-9108-25G or UCSX-I-9108-100G, two X-Fabric slots. Typical compute: UCSX-210c-M6/M7, UCSX-410c-M7; parent FIs often UCS 6500 series. Requires UCSM 4.2+ for XML class polling (Splunk 2731). IMM/Intersight-only domains: use UC-19.1.22, not this UCSM IFM path.
